channel.title: Cariboo Signals
channel.copyright: © 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
channel.itunes:subtitle: Technology and society in rural BC with Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:author: Erich Zirnhelt
channel.itunes:summary:
How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Riley brings tech systems thinking with rural roots, while Casey focuses on community development. New episodes every day with weekly themes.
Community Accountability: Cariboo Signals scores 18/20 (Exemplary) on the Community Content Compact — Track A (Community-Insider production). Transparency: 4/4 · Source Integrity: 3/3 · Displacement: 2/2 · Consent & Attribution: 3/4 · Benefit Flow: 4/5 · Accountability: 2/2. Produced by Erich Zirnhelt, Horsefly, BC (Secwépemc territory). Corrections and PSA opt-outs: feedback@cariboosignals.ca.
channel.description: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Community Content Compact: 18/20 Exemplary (Track A). Self-funded, non-commercial. Producer: Erich Zirnhelt, Horsefly, BC. Corrections: feedback@cariboosignals.ca.

trace:assessment.trace:community: Rural Cariboo region, BC — Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh territories

item.title: Science, Wonder And The Natural World
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Science, Wonder & the Natural World.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [ScienceAlert] Your Blood May Contain an Evolutionary Relic Older Than Animals Themselves and [ScienceDaily] Adorable tiny blue octopus found nearly 6,000 feet beneath the Galápagos, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What does it mean for ecological management and knowledge systems when living organisms demonstrate capabilities and strategies more sophisticated than our scientific models predict, and who benefits from the discovery? Topics include: Carnivorous plant catch-and-release strategy and evolutionary optimization, Sea cucumber cellular autonomy and tissue regeneration without central nervous system, Indigenous knowledge systems vs. Western scientific discovery frameworks, Research funding models and institutional incentives for long-term regional presence, Science communication infrastructure gaps in rural Cariboo region.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Loaves & Fishes Outreach

Sources discussed:

  • Michelle Starr (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] Your Blood May Contain an Evolutionary Relic ...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Adorable tiny blue octopus found nearly 6,000...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Deadly fungus and lung parasites are hammerin...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists discover hidden gut-brain circuit ...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists discover why Ozempic and Wegovy we...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists discover ancient single-celled anc...
  • Michelle Starr (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] Spacetime 'Crystals' Could Collapse Into Tiny...
  • The Conversation (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] Watch: Immune Cells Caught on Video Eating Li...
  • Jess Cockerill (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] This Plant Summons Wasps When Under Attack, A...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] This prehistoric fish may explain how animals...
  • Jess Cockerill (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] 'Mammoth' Bones Kept in a Museum For 70 Years...
  • The Conversation (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] A Single Dose of Psilocybin Could Ease Depres...

Additional sources provided:

  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Queenless wasp colonies explode into chaos bu...
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] Schrödinger’s Kittens Are All Grown Up
  • David Nield (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] A Common Vitamin Helps Cancer Defend Itself –...
  • David Nield (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] Scientists Discover New Giant Virus That Repl...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Massive supercomputer simulations unlock cosm...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists discover why some DNA-doubled cell...
  • https://refractor.io/author/bronwyn-thompson/ (New Atlas): [New Atlas] Brain-cell breakthrough supercharges GLP-1 weigh...
  • Carly Cassella (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] A Dazzling Meteor Just 'Video Bombed' a Volca...
  • https://refractor.io/author/bronwyn-thompson/ (New Atlas): [New Atlas] This plant could be the smartest carnivore on th...
  • https://refractor.io/author/mike-mcrae/ (New Atlas): [New Atlas] Sea creature's amputated limbs refused to die, e...

Closing Music: “She Knows” by JARA — indie, pop, rocknroll. Free music via Jamendo, licensed under Creative Commons.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
item.itunes:summary:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Science, Wonder & the Natural World.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [ScienceAlert] Your Blood May Contain an Evolutionary Relic Older Than Animals Themselves and [ScienceDaily] Adorable tiny blue octopus found nearly 6,000 feet beneath the Galápagos, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What does it mean for ecological management and knowledge systems when living organisms demonstrate capabilities and strategies more sophisticated than our scientific models predict, and who benefits from the discovery? Topics include: Carnivorous plant catch-and-release strategy and evolutionary optimization, Sea cucumber cellular autonomy and tissue regeneration without central nervous system, Indigenous knowledge systems vs. Western scientific discovery frameworks, Research funding models and institutional incentives for long-term regional presence, Science communication infrastructure gaps in rural Cariboo region.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Cariboo Local Affairs
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Local Affairs.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down the week's top tech developments, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can municipal enforcement tools ('drastic measures') effectively address prolific offending in small cities, or do solutions require provincial-level intervention? Topics include: Municipal jurisdiction limitations versus provincial control of criminal justice and service provision, Integrated response teams and evidence-based models (Medicine Hat, Foundry Cariboo Chilcotin, ACT teams), The accountability gap between what decision-makers hear and what they document/act on, Cumulative service contraction in small cities (airline loss, healthcare pressures, concurrent service gaps), The role of municipal politicians as translators of specific needs to senior government.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Quesnel Women's Resource Centre

Sources discussed:

  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Williams Lake City Councillor calls for dra...

Closing Music: “Give Me Hope” by Modern Pitch — rock, pop, indie. Free music via Jamendo, licensed under Creative Commons.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-opus-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
item.itunes:summary:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Local Affairs.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down the week's top tech developments, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can municipal enforcement tools ('drastic measures') effectively address prolific offending in small cities, or do solutions require provincial-level intervention? Topics include: Municipal jurisdiction limitations versus provincial control of criminal justice and service provision, Integrated response teams and evidence-based models (Medicine Hat, Foundry Cariboo Chilcotin, ACT teams), The accountability gap between what decision-makers hear and what they document/act on, Cumulative service contraction in small cities (airline loss, healthcare pressures, concurrent service gaps), The role of municipal politicians as translators of specific needs to senior government.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Quesnel Women's Resource Centre

Sources discussed:

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Cariboo communities to host local government leadership workshops ahead of 2026 elections and [Williams Lake Tribune] Quesnel caught off guard by Pacific Coastal service suspension, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can general-purpose AI tools reliably identify wildlife and assist with land stewardship in underrepresented geographic regions like the Cariboo, or do they require regional validation and community-controlled governance frameworks? Topics include: Regional accuracy gaps in AI training datasets for underrepresented geographies, Distinction between purpose-built ecological tools vs. general-purpose AI extended into outdoor contexts, Indigenous data sovereignty and Tŝilhqot'in territorial knowledge governance, Community science contribution to ecological baselines (iNaturalist, trail building), Risk of treating AI outputs as certainty rather than hypothesis in safety-critical contexts like wildlife identification.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: First Journey Trails & Indigenous Youth Mountain Bike Program

Sources discussed:

  • Laisa Conde (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Cariboo communities to host local go...
  • Laisa Conde (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Quesnel caught off guard by Pacific ...
  • Laisa Conde (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Spring GoByBike is back May 30 to Ju...
  • Patrick Davies (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Cariboo-Prince George MP Todd Dohert...
  • Laisa Conde (Quesnel Cariboo Observer): [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Pacific Coastal Airlines ending Q...
  • rebeccadyok (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake’s gymnast memory and l...
  • Laisa Conde (Quesnel Cariboo Observer): [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Whole Child Early Learning Centre...
  • Android Authority: [Android Authority] Gemini’s camera AI thinks Aussie wildlif...

Additional sources provided:

  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Two Langley councillors drop libel l...
  • none (Quesnel Cariboo Observer): [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Notice of Bankruptcy
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘Voice of the cathedral’: B.C. churc...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] VIDEO: Fatal Langley shooting is sam...
  • none (100 Mile Free Press): [100 Mile Free Press] CARIBOO OUTDOORS: A couple of days tro...
  • Brittany Webster (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. wildfire documentary hits YouTu...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6

© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [CBC Kamloops] Sacred fire at Williams Lake First Nation marks 5 years since Tk'emlúps te Secwépemc announcement and [IndigiNews] In Kjipuktuk, Indigenous photojournalism fellows end week with emotion-filled ceremony, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What does it take for Indigenous communities to build and control their own technology infrastructure rather than relying on external vendors and advisory roles? Topics include: Data sovereignty infrastructure ownership vs. advisory roles, OCAP principles and the gap between endorsement and implementation, Indigenous AI governance: advisory circles and decision-making authority, Cultural knowledge encoding in technology and intellectual property limitations, Shared vs. individual community infrastructure models and accountability structures.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Quesnel Tillicum Society / Native Friendship Centre

Sources discussed:

  • CBC Kamloops: [CBC Kamloops] Sacred fire at Williams Lake First Nation mar...
  • Cara McKenna (IndigiNews): [IndigiNews] In Kjipuktuk, Indigenous photojournalism fellow...
  • Aaron Hemens, Local Journalism Initiative Reporter (IndigiNews): [IndigiNews] syilx Okanagan Nation declares watershed emerge...
  • none (100 Mile Free Press): [100 Mile Free Press] Yvonne Marie Simpson
  • Newsletter: n***@animikii.com: New: Indigenous AI Advisory Circle
  • Newsletter: n***@animikii.com: Māori-owned data storage network hailed as significant step ...

Additional sources provided:

  • CFJC Today Kamloops: [CFJC Today Kamloops] B.C. Indigenous relations face ‘fraugh...
  • Jeremy Appel, Local Journalism Initiative Reporter, Chevi Rabbit, Local Journalism Initiative Report (IndigiNews): [IndigiNews] After court win, First Nations denounce ‘Albert...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Liǧʷiłdax̌ʷ members protest against ...
  • The Narwhal: [The Narwhal] Grassy Narrows seeks to appeal Ontario mine pe...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Pilot error blamed for 2023 northwes...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/john-velasco (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] I drove the Subaru Trailseeker for a week — and...
  • https://www.techradar.com/author/sead-fadilpai (TechRadar): [TechRadar] Worrying open-source security issue 'BadHost' co...
  • Josh Kozelj (The Tyee): [The Tyee] Would You Eat a Salmon Fillet Grown in a Lab?
  • https://www.techradar.com/author/efosa-udinmwen (TechRadar): [TechRadar] 'Self-reporting is the best way we can get this ...
  • https://www.tomshardware.com/author/jon-martindale (Tom's Hardware): [Tom's Hardware] AI costs begin to bite as agents may increa...
  • Newsletter: n***@animikii.com: Preserving Kwakwaka'wakw carving traditions through rob...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6

Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘Will miss caring for you’: Clearwater losing 4 doctors, recruitment efforts underway and [TechCrunch] What ClickUp’s mass layoff tells us about the future of work, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can working land operations in the Cariboo maintain long-term viability and succession when land financialization and investor ownership create structural incentives to sell rather than continue farming? Topics include: Land financialization and investor ownership of agricultural operations, Succession planning gaps in working land operations, Alternative ownership models: collective/Indigenous land tenure versus individual ownership, Producer data sovereignty and precision agriculture as competitive differentiation, Financing structures and credit union products designed for agricultural succession rather than liquidation.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Mt. Timothy Ski Society

Sources discussed:

  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘Will miss caring for you’: Clearwat...
  • Marina Temkin (TechCrunch): [TechCrunch] What ClickUp’s mass layoff tells us about the f...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] A Cattle Ranch Is Doing What Ivy League Co...

Additional sources provided:

  • Global News: [Global News] Consultations with Sask. producers ‘an opportu...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Taseko Mines Ltd. exploring name cha...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. fire crew rescues ducklings fro...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] No charges approved in 2023 Vancouve...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Saanich Police seize $55K worth of f...
  • Simon Spichak (Ricochet): [Ricochet] Inside the black box reshaping Canada’s benefits ...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Low Head Turbine Generates Plenty of Power
  • Jeremy White (WIRED): [WIRED] The Electric Ferrari Luce Is Finally Here
  • https://www.techradar.com/author/leon-poultney (TechRadar): [TechRadar] I’ve driven the smallest Kia EV yet and there’s ...
  • Andrew J. Hawkins (The Verge): [The Verge] Why Nuro thinks being a robotaxi ‘second mover’ ...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] How solo founders use AI to reduce the op...
  • Isaac Phan Nay (The Tyee): [The Tyee] A Very Shiny Debt Trap
  • The Narwhal: [The Narwhal] For many Canadian farmers, selling land can be...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/alice-carroll (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] When to fertilize tomatoes: the 3-step schedule...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)

  • https://www.techradar.com/author/leon-poultney (TechRadar): [TechRadar] I’ve driven the smallest Kia EV yet and there’s ...
  • Andrew J. Hawkins (The Verge): [The Verge] Why Nuro thinks being a robotaxi ‘second mover’ ...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] How solo founders use AI to reduce the op...
  • Isaac Phan Nay (The Tyee): [The Tyee] A Very Shiny Debt Trap
  • The Narwhal: [The Narwhal] For many Canadian farmers, selling land can be...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/alice-carroll (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] When to fertilize tomatoes: the 3-step schedule...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Engadget] Spotify is launching verification badges for podcasts to help listeners avoid AI slop and [The Verge] AI video is moving beyond clip slop, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can technical transparency standards like TRACE effectively govern AI-generated cultural content, or do they require deeper institutional and legislative frameworks to be meaningful? Topics include: TRACE standard components (Transparency, Responsibility, Accountability, Consent, Explainability), Difference between transparency disclosure and authorization consent in cultural contexts, Indigenous data sovereignty and tiered access requirements (Mukurtu systems, OCAP principles), Implementation gaps in standards adoption (auditing infrastructure, platform enforcement), Community-scale accountability versus formal regulatory frameworks.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Central Cariboo Arts & Culture Society

Sources discussed:

  • Lawrence Bonk (Engadget): [Engadget] Spotify is launching verification badges for podc...
  • Janko Roettgers (The Verge): [The Verge] AI video is moving beyond clip slop
  • Ivan Mehta (TechCrunch): [TechCrunch] Spotify adds AI-powered Q&A and briefing genera...
  • Rob Beschizza (Boing Boing): [Boing Boing] 16 bytes of code generate this video and the m...
  • https://newatlas.com/author/david-szondy/ (New Atlas): [New Atlas] Hybrid Texas power plant blends the best of gas ...
  • Shanal Otchere (Resilience.org): [Resilience.org] Seeds Series Volume 2: Building regenerativ...
  • https://www.tomshardware.com/author/etiido-uko (Tom's Hardware): [Tom's Hardware] Huawei claims sanctions-busting breakthroug...

Additional sources provided:

  • NPR Health News: [NPR Health News] Overworked and understaffed: Special ed te...
  • David Nield (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] One Dietary Supplement Was Found to Lower Agg...
  • Lily Hay Newman (WIRED): [WIRED] The AI Era Is Creating a Bug Hunting Arms Race
  • https://www.techradar.com/author/james-barker (TechRadar): [TechRadar] Why fiber is the real secret to scaling intellig...
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: #1366 Death of a Journalist: Remembering Elaine Dewar
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: #182 The Canadian Defence Gold Rush
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: Discover Our Cell Phone Plans | Fizz

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Engadget] Spotify is launching verification badges for podcasts to help listeners avoid AI slop and [The Verge] AI video is moving beyond clip slop, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can technical transparency standards like TRACE effectively govern AI-generated cultural content, or do they require deeper institutional and legislative frameworks to be meaningful? Topics include: TRACE standard components (Transparency, Responsibility, Accountability, Consent, Explainability), Difference between transparency disclosure and authorization consent in cultural contexts, Indigenous data sovereignty and tiered access requirements (Mukurtu systems, OCAP principles), Implementation gaps in standards adoption (auditing infrastructure, platform enforcement), Community-scale accountability versus formal regulatory frameworks.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).
